title: DEI to DIE Act
plain_summary: The DEI to DIE Act would force all government agencies to stop their diversity, equity, and inclusion programs within two months. These programs are meant to help make workplaces more fair for people of different backgrounds. The agencies would have to tell Congress how much money they spent on these programs. Government officials would also have to meet every month to make sure the programs are being ended. The bill would completely remove these types of programs from the federal government.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
subjects: Government employee pay, benefits, personnel management

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the DEI to DIE Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Elimination of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion in Federal Government
sections.text:
Congress believes the following things. The Biden Administration created programs called diversity, equity, and inclusion programs (called DEI programs in this law) that illegally discriminated against people. These programs were put into almost every part of the federal government, from airline safety to the military. This effort started on President Biden's first day in office when he signed Executive Order thirteen thousand nine hundred eighty five called Advancing Racial Equity and Support for Underserved Communities Through the Federal Government. Because of this order and similar orders, almost every federal agency created Equity Action Plans that explained how they would use DEI programs in the federal government. When these plans were made public, they showed huge waste of public money and shameful discrimination. This ends today. Americans deserve a government that serves every person with equal dignity and respect and that only spends taxpayer money on making America great.

The Director of the Office of Management and Budget (called OMB in this law), with help from the Attorney General and the Director of the Office of Personnel Management (called OPM in this law), must work together to end all discriminatory programs. This includes all illegal diversity, equity, and inclusion programs (called DEI in this law) and diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility programs (called DEIA in this law). They must end all mandates, policies, programs, preferences, and activities in the federal government, no matter what they are called. To do this, the Director of OPM, with help from the Attorney General when needed, must review and change all existing federal employment practices, union contracts, and training policies or programs to follow this law. Federal employment practices, including how federal employees are evaluated, must reward individual effort, skills, performance, and hard work. They cannot consider DEI or DEIA factors, goals, policies, mandates, or requirements under any circumstances.

Each agency, department, or commission head must work with the Attorney General, the Director of OMB, and the Director of OPM to take these actions within sixty days after this law is passed. They must end all DEI, DEIA, and environmental justice offices and positions (including Chief Diversity Officer positions). They must end all equity action plans, equity actions, initiatives, or programs, equity related grants or contracts, and all DEI or DEIA performance requirements for employees, contractors, or people who receive grants. They must give the Director of OMB a list of all agency or department DEI, DEIA, or environmental justice positions, committees, programs, services, activities, budgets, and spending that existed on November fourth, two thousand twenty four. They must also assess whether these positions, committees, programs, services, activities, budgets, and spending have been renamed in a misleading way to keep their original function. They must list all federal contractors who provided DEI training or DEI training materials to agency or department employees. They must list all federal grant recipients who received federal money to provide or advance DEI, DEIA, or environmental justice programs, services, or activities since January twentieth, two thousand twenty one. They must direct the deputy agency or department head to assess the operational impact (including the number of new DEI hires) and cost of the prior administration's DEI, DEIA, and environmental justice programs and policies. The deputy must also recommend actions, such as congressional notifications under section five hundred thirty D of title twenty eight of the United States Code, to align agency or department programs, activities, policies, regulations, guidance, employment practices, enforcement activities, contracts (including set asides), grants, consent orders, and legal positions with the policy of equal dignity and respect identified in this law. The agency or department head and the Director of OMB must work together to make sure the deputy agency or department head has the authority and resources needed to carry out this law.

To inform and advise the President so he can create appropriate and effective civil rights policies for the Executive Branch, the Assistant to the President for Domestic Policy must hold a monthly meeting. The Director of OMB, the Director of OPM, and each deputy agency or department head must attend these meetings. At these meetings they will hear reports on how common DEI, DEIA, and environmental justice programs are and what they cost economically and socially in agency or department programs, activities, policies, regulations, guidance, employment practices, enforcement activities, contracts (including set asides), grants, consent orders, and legal positions. They will discuss any barriers to measures to comply with this law. They will monitor and track agency and department progress and identify potential areas for additional Presidential or legislative action to advance the policy of equal dignity and respect.

If any part of this law or how it applies to any person or situation is found to be invalid, the rest of this law and how it applies to other persons or circumstances will not be affected.
